section_title: A contract by which one party promises to save the other from loss
content:
124. A contract by which one party promises to save the other from loss
indemnity"
caused to him by the conduct of the promisor himself, or by the conduct of
defined
any other person, is called a "contract of indemnity".
Illustration
A contracts to indemnify B against the consequences of any proceedings
which C may take against B in respect of a certain sum of 200 Taka. This is a
contract of indemnity.
